Root Cause (first observed failure):

action.hypothesis: `residual_topk` still has no quality signal because the latest prototype attempt failed at the same architecture-selection gate before training began. The smallest informative move remains a narrow code-fix round inside `sparsify/` that explicitly registers `residual_topk` in config validation and SAE class dispatch, so the intended K=128 proxy prototype can finally clear selection and reach a real sanity/training signal.
action.summary: Fix `residual_topk` architecture registration and dispatch under `sparsify/`, then rerun the same K=128 proxy prototype under the current signum baseline.
action.self_review: This keeps the round focused on the repeated blocker instead of spending another cycle on exhausted gated sweeps. The main risk is that registration is only the first missing piece, but resolving the concrete selection failure is still the cleanest next step and preserves comparability of the intended prototype run.
notes_to_memory: Round 61 repeated the same `Unknown architecture: 'residual_topk'` failure, so the family is still blocked at architecture registration rather than judged on quality or runtime.
The stable gated frontier remains useful as an anchor, but further local gated sweeps are currently less informative than unblocking a new family that still has no valid proxy signal.
This round should remain a minimal blocker-removal pass limited to making `residual_topk` selectable and constructible by the existing execution layer.
next_hypotheses: If `residual_topk` clears sanity and yields a competitive proxy result, keep the family alive and tune its internal allocation next.
If `residual_topk` runs but is clearly uncompetitive, archive it quickly and move to a different architecture family.

If the run still fails after registration and dispatch are fixed, treat the remaining issue as deeper architecture compatibility and switch to another new family rather than returning to more gated local tuning.
